description: §16.2 Workflow-tier dashboard 2/5 — Dagster asset materialisation health. Reads from the unified workflow.workflow_runs view + the bronze/silver/gold asset lineage Dagster publishes via OpenLineage.

panels.title: Asset run latency p95 by tier (s)
panels.description: How long each asset materialisation took. Bronze (raw ingest) typically <60s; silver (joins + canonicalisation) <300s; gold (analytics) <900s. Sustained p95 above these bands = upstream slowdown.

panels.title: Asset materialisation success rate (%)
panels.description: Per-asset success counter divided by total runs. Sustained <95% on any single asset is alert-worthy.

panels.title: Asset freshness (minutes since last materialisation)
panels.description: How long since each asset was last successfully materialised. Stale gold assets break dashboards that depend on them. Materialise-on-schedule policy: bronze hourly, silver daily, gold daily.

panels.title: data_version bumps by store (events/h)
panels.description: Tracks silver.workspaces.data_version and silver.projects.data_version increments. Each Dagster ingest run that produces new rows should bump these — they're the freshness signal that busts the retrieval cache (Global Invariant 12).

panels.title: Failed materialisations last 24h
panels.description: Any non-zero count warrants a triage. Cross-reference with audit.audit_ledger where action_type='asset.materialization_failed'.

panels.title: Rows ingested last 24h (bronze)
panels.description: Sum across all bronze asset materialisations. Indicates the platform is actively absorbing new data.

panels.title: Bronze→silver→gold lag (median, min)
panels.description: Median lag between bronze row insert and the matching silver/gold materialisation. Sustained > 1 hr means the schedule cadence isn't keeping up.

panels.title: RUN_FAILURE rule active?
panels.description: Dagster sensor for the RUN_FAILURE event subscription. Should be 1 (active) at all times. 0 = sensor offline; new failures won't escalate.

title: GeoRAG — Workflows (Dagster assets)
